key_insight: A multihead physics-informed neural network can learn a low-dimensional latent manifold of PDE solution families, revealing robust principal directions and frequency structure beyond the initial condition.
engineering_relevance: This offers a compact, reproducible way to model and analyze parameterized PDE solution spaces for surrogate simulation, reduced-order modeling, and spectral diagnostics.
method: Shared PINN body learns a latent embedding of the solution manifold while linear heads reconstruct solutions for different initial conditions.
A head-orthogonalization penalty removes latent degeneracies and stabilizes principal-component spectra across training runs.
Fourier-shell analysis attributes frequency-band contributions to each principal component in a basis-invariant way.
evidence_signal: On the 1D viscous Burgers equation, a latent dimension of 20 compressed to about 2–4 principal components for ~95% variance and 4–7 for ~99%, with similar qualitative results for heat and wave equations.
watch_item: Because the initial condition is built into the output by construction, the learned principal components describe only additional variability; results may also depend on the chosen orthogonalization strength and latent dimension.
paper_title: Physics-Informed Neural Embeddings of PDE Solution Families
